title: Press Release | For over a decade, the SDGs have delivered results – now the world must urgently scale up what works, UN report finds
content:
PRESS RELEASE UN Secretary-General António Guterres announces Prime Minister of Spain as new Co-Chair of SDG Advocates group NEW YORK, 15 July 2026–Amidst the growing number of development crises facing the world, United Nations Secretary-General António Guterres today announced Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez of Spain as Co-Chair of the Sustainable Development Goals Advocates group alongside [...]

The post Press Release | For over a decade, the SDGs have delivered results – now the world must urgently scale up what works, UN report finds appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: SDG Media Zone at the 2026 HLPF
content:
The High-level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) is the central United Nations platform for the follow-up and review of the implementation of the  Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) . On the sidelines of the Forum, the SDG Media Zone  is an opportunity for world leaders from all sectors to discuss, collaborate, and share innovative ideas for [...]

The post SDG Media Zone at the 2026 HLPF appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.
snippet: The High-level Political Forum on Sustainable Development (HLPF) is the central United Nations platform for the follow-up and review of the implementation of the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) .
creator: Isabel Gontijo Hamdan

title: World must move faster on Ebola response for DR Congo: UN relief chief
content:
The world must move faster to contain the spread of Ebola in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), UN relief chief Tom Fletcher said on Thursday. Read Full Story on UN News

The post World must move faster on Ebola response for DR Congo: UN relief chief appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Restoring dignity: Senegalese surgeon and Burundian foundation win reproductive health awards
content:
A Senegalese surgeon who has dedicated his career to restoring the health and dignity of women living with obstetric fistula, and a Burundian foundation supporting vulnerable women and children, have been named the winners of the 2026 United Nations Population Award. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Restoring dignity: Senegalese surgeon and Burundian foundation win reproductive health awards appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

content:

title: Southwest Pacific’s last tropical glacier could vanish within months
content:
The Southwest Pacific is facing mounting climate risks as its vast ocean expanse becomes hotter, more acidic and more dangerous for coastal communities, the World Meteorological Organization (WMO) said in a report published on Tuesday.   Read Full Story on UN News

The post Southwest Pacific’s last tropical glacier could vanish within months appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.
snippet: The Southwest Pacific is facing mounting climate risks as its vast ocean expanse becomes hotter, more acidic and more dangerous for coastal communities, the World Meteorological Organization (WMO)

title: Ebola continues to spread in DRC as death toll passes 500, WHO warns
content:
The outbreak of the deadly Bundibugyo species of Ebola in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is expanding, while the push to accelerate testing and identify effective treatment options continues, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) said on Tuesday. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Ebola continues to spread in DRC as death toll passes 500, WHO warns appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: Venezuela quake: UN continues to scale up as damage estimate reaches $37 billion
content:
The UN and its partners are ramping up assistance for people affected by the earthquakes in Venezuela, working alongside the Government's response effort, as a new UN estimate puts direct physical damage at $37 billion. Read Full Story on UN News

The post Venezuela quake: UN continues to scale up as damage estimate reaches $37 billion appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.

title: A safe return home: Tackling stigma on Uganda’s Ebola frontline
content:
As Uganda works to contain an Ebola outbreak that is also affecting neighbouring Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), health workers are doing more than responding to a deadly virus. They are helping families navigate fear, misinformation and stigma.  Read Full Story on UN News

The post A safe return home: Tackling stigma on Uganda’s Ebola frontline appeared first on United Nations Sustainable Development.
